item.text: Using the `proposal` skill because the request names it and asks for a change proposal. I’ll stay within the fixture’s project-local files and avoid RigorLoop internal docs.

item.aggregated_output:
# Customer Fixture Workflow Guide

## Customer-project portability

Public skills operate in customer-project mode by default.

Use project-local artifacts when present and relevant.

Do not require RigorLoop repository-internal specs, docs, root governance, reports, or follow-up files in this customer fixture.

When local guidance is absent, use portable defaults where safe and block on ambiguity where no safe default exists.

## Artifact locations

| Artifact | Location |
|---|---|
| specs | `specs/` |
| plans | `docs/plans/` |
| change root | `docs/changes/<change-id>/` |

docs/changes/example-change/explain-change.md
docs/changes/example-change/change.yaml
docs/workflows.md
docs/plans/customer-plan.md
specs/customer-feature.md
rigorloop.yaml
tests/app.test.js
rigorloop.lock
src/app.js

item.text: `docs/workflows.md` exists and gives fixture-specific portability guidance plus artifact locations. Root `VISION.md` was absent, so I’m checking only the current app behavior to ground the proposal summary without expanding into unrelated artifacts.

- evidence used: user request; `docs/workflows.md`; absence of root `VISION.md`; `src/app.js`; `tests/app.test.js`; current date `2026-05-18`
- whether local `docs/workflows.md` was used: yes
- portable default or ambiguity behavior: `docs/workflows.md` has no proposal location, so use the proposal skill portable default; root `VISION.md` absence is a substantive-proposal blocker
- attempted reliance on absent RigorLoop internals: no
- proposed proposal path: `docs/proposals/2026-05-18-greeting-suffix-option.md`
- one-paragraph proposal summary: Add an optional greeting suffix so callers can keep the current `hello <name>` output by default while opting into a suffix such as punctuation or a short phrase. The recommended direction is a backward-compatible function option rather than changing the default greeting or adding global configuration, with tests covering unchanged default behavior and explicit suffix behavior. Since root `VISION.md` is absent, this should not proceed as a substantive proposal until the vision gate is resolved or the work is explicitly treated as bootstrap-exempt.
